title: pyrrhocactus horridus
intro: Pyrrhocactus horridus, also known as the fiery stick cactus, is a type of flowering cactus native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant is known for its vibrant red and yellow flowers, which bloom at the end of summer. The pyrrhocactus horridus has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it a potential remedy for wound healing and skin issues. Herbal preparations of pyrrhocactus horridus may be used in teas, tinctures, or salves to harness its medicinal benefits, which include promoting skin health and reducing inflammation.
